behavior_summary: U8P_WEB::gotHit executes when the object receives a hit; it records a small piece of info on the hit object and then removes (destroys) that object.

The handler pushes a constant info key (0x20C), a 2-byte indirect value (from indirect_02), and the object's pointer (BP+06) to execute a 'set info' on the object, then performs a 'process exclude' operation and finally calls Item::destroy on the same object.

This sequence appears designed to mark or record who/what hit the object (or to set a small state/flag) immediately prior to removing the object from the world, ensuring the info is stored before destruction.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: U8P_WEB::gotHit is run in response to the U8P_WEB::gotHit event (Event 6) and runs to completion in one process invocation.
Deterministic linear flow: set info (with key 0x20C) -> process exclude -> Item::destroy. There are no observed branches or loops in this handler.
The 'process exclude' step is invoked after writing info and before destruction, likely to alter scheduling/processing of the object so it will not be further processed while being removed.
open_questions: What exact semantics does info key 0x20C encode (attacker id, damage type, quest flag, or other metadata)?
What is the precise meaning and scope of the pushed 2-byte indirect value (indirect_02) — attacker PID, damage amount, or a small encoded flag?
What exact effect does 'process exclude' have in the engine for this object (prevents further AI, hides from lists, defers callbacks, etc.)?
Are there any side-effects elsewhere in the game triggered by setting info 0x20C prior to destruction?

main_function.name: U8P_WEB::gotHit
main_function.purpose: On being hit, record a small info entry (key 0x20C) on the hit object (value from an indirect 2-byte source) and then destroy that object.
entry_conditions: Event 6 (U8P_WEB::gotHit) — invoked when this object is struck or receives a hit.

function_map.name: U8P_WEB::gotHit
function_map.role: Handle U8P_WEB::gotHit event: set object info and destroy the object

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Engine routine to remove/destroy an item/object instance

external_calls.purpose: Remove/destroy the hit object passed at BP+06 after recording info

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_WEB::gotHit executes when the object receives a hit; it records a small piece of info on the hit object and then removes (destroys) that object.
